desc: Curated example DAG illustrating the protocol shape: scrub cluster (rip cruft) -> curate cluster (clean remaining content) -> verify cluster (pre-flip gates) -> terminal (flip + tag) -> plan-mode child (post-launch contribution surface). This is the public-release prep round for the roadmap engine itself, retained as a worked example. Clusters group by concern; depends:[] is the only sequencing. The terminal is plan-mode because its concrete shape depends on launch feedback that does not yet exist when the spec is authored.

init.desc: Round entry. Confirm session state: release branch checked out, no in-flight uncommitted work. The receipt names the operator and time and pins the starting head.

s-author-leak-scanner.desc: Author scripts/leak-scan.js — a Node module that reads a gitignored sidecar of leak patterns and scans tracked files for unallowlisted hits. Exit 0 on clean; exit 1 with file:line :: pattern lines on dirty. Single source of leak-policy across all downstream validators.

s-strip-defensive-wrappers.desc: Delete the defensive-wrapper layer that exists only because of single-maintainer multi-repo development (clone-role enforcement, fs read wrappers, hardcoded-path checks). Public contributors should see fs.readFileSync, not a 200-LOC wrapper around it. Sweep call sites; verify no residual references in src/ or tests/.

s-strip-husky.desc: Remove the husky commit-msg hook that walls off contributors with a maintainer-specific message format. Replace with a CI workflow that lints commit messages on PRs only. Drop the husky devDependency.

s-purge-private-files.desc: Delete maintainer-specific files that should never have been committed: skill templates tied to other-project doctrine, internal design notes, mirror-sync hooks with hardcoded paths. Validator: leak-scan over the working tree exits 0.

c-license.desc: Add LICENSE (MIT). Update package.json license field to match. The repo currently has no license file, which blocks downstream adoption.

c-package-metadata.desc: Curate package.json for public discoverability: name, description, repository URL, keywords, author, license. The current install instruction in README points at someone else's package; this node fixes the source of truth.

c-scrub-readme.desc: Rewrite README.md for a public reader: what the tool is, install (correct package name), one quickstart example (roadmap make + orient + advance), pointer to the example DAG under .roadmap/heads/. Remove maintainer-internal references.

c-curate-roadmap-example.desc: Replace live dogfooding state under .roadmap/ with one curated example DAG plus a README. Live runtime state (handoffs, trail, completed, round receipts) stays gitignored. The example is the artifact a public reader sees first; it must be small and leak-clean.

v-leak-sweep.desc: Run leak-scan over every tracked file in the working tree. Single gate before flipping the repo public.

v-fresh-install.desc: Simulate a fresh contributor: clone into a tmpdir, pnpm install, pnpm test, run the documented quickstart from README. Catches missing files, wrong package names, and skill-template residue.

term-flip-public-and-tag.desc: Terminal: flip repo visibility to public via gh CLI and tag v0.2.0. Both v-leak-sweep and v-fresh-install must be GREEN before this node runs.

p-post-launch-contribution.desc: Plan-mode terminal: full contribution surface (CONTRIBUTING.md, issue triage labels, PR review process, Discussions). Concrete shape depends on launch feedback that does not yet exist; this node decomposes into children once that signal arrives.
